details.calculation: ($54,800,000 / 140 total beneficiaries) = $391,428.57 per outcome. | A $1,000 donation achieves ≈ 0.00255 outcomes.

details.elaboration: Quote: '1,760 street cats were desexed and vaccinated for free under our Cat Colony Care Programme.'
details.formula: Highest level of evidence cited in impact claims

details.calculation: A quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

metrics.context_qualifier: Total animals desexed through various programs in 2023/24, aiming to stabilize animal population in the community.
metrics.evidence_quote: 11,086 animals were spayed/ neutered through our desexing services or under our animal birth control welfare scheme that aims to stabilise the animal population in the community.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total animals collected, rescued, surrendered, or selected for rehoming by the Inspectorate and other departments in 2023/24.
Street cats desexed and vaccinated for free under the Cat Colony Care Programme in 2023/24.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without the program, more cats would have been euthanized, as indicated by historical trends.

metrics.evidence_quote: 1,760 street cats were desexed and vaccinated for free under our Cat Colony Care Programme.
metrics.metric_name: Street Cats Desexed (Cat Colony Care Programme)

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs desexed under the Community Dog Programme in 2023/24 to regulate population and improve well-being.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without the program, more dogs would have been euthanized, as indicated by historical trends.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals adopted and placed in new loving homes in 2023/24.
metrics.evidence_quote: 979 animals were adopted and became cherished family members of their new loving homes.

metrics.context_qualifier: Investigations effectively carried out by the Inspectorate Department in 2023/24 to prevent animal suffering.
metrics.evidence_quote: 1,342 animal cruelty and animal trading investigations were effectively carried out to prevent animal suffering.

metrics.context_qualifier: SNAP vouchers provided to the public for desexing services in 83 participating veterinary clinics in 2023/24.
metrics.evidence_quote: 1,592 SNAP vouchers were provided to the public for use in 83 participating veterinary clinics for desexing services.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals receiving dedicated care through the Foster Parent Programme in 2023/24.
metrics.evidence_quote: 672 animals benefited through the Foster Parent Programme and received dedicated care from our foster parents on their journey to finding a forever home.
metrics.metric_name: Animals Benefited from Foster Parent Programme

Number of cats euthanised in 2023, showing a significant decrease from previous years due to desexing programs.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Euthanasia rates were much higher before desexing programs, e.g., 6310 cats in 1997.

metrics.evidence_quote: Cats neutered under the CCCP Vs. cat euthanasia rate

metrics.evidence_quote: Dogs neutered under the MDP Vs. dog euthanasia rate

significant_events.event_name: Advocacy for Animal Feeding Ban and Legislation Amendments
significant_events.source_quote: Of these - albeit coming into force in August 2024 - is the extension of feeding bans to cover feral pigeons and the introduction of fixed penalty notices. The team will also continue to focus on advocacy to regulate trade linked to animals under Cap.139 Public Health (Animals and Birds) Ordinance, and in particular the trade of cats and exotics.
significant_events.summary: Lobbied for extension of feeding bans for feral pigeons and introduction of fixed penalty notices (effective Aug 2024). Advocated for amendments to Cap. 169 Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Ordinance and regulation of trade under Cap. 139 Public Health (Animals and Birds) Ordinance.

significant_events.event_name: Opening of SPCA Jockey Club Centennial Centre (Tsing Yi Centre)
significant_events.source_quote: The energy and excitement that we all felt culminated in the move to the SPCA Jockey Club Centennial Centre in Tsing Yi at the end of the calendar year for some of our staff, leading up to the grand opening in March 2024.
significant_events.summary: Grand opening in March 2024, significantly expanding capacity for animal treatment, shelter, desexing operations, adoption, and education. Consolidated operations from other facilities.

significant_events.source_quote: Our Animal Birth Control Programmes are critical in preventing and alleviating as much suffering as we can and addressing companion animal overpopulation at its root.
significant_events.summary: Continued desexing services for free-roaming and stray animals (Cat Colony Care Programme, Community Dog Programme) to manage and control animal populations in Hong Kong.

significant_events.event_name: Animal Rescue and Cruelty Prevention Services
significant_events.source_quote: Today, the 20-person team carries out a wide range of tasks which most famously includes a 24-hour rescue service.
significant_events.summary: Operated a 24-hour rescue service, conducted animal cruelty and trading investigations, and assisted law enforcement in prosecuting offenders across Hong Kong.

significant_events.event_name: Launch of Adoption Date App and Events
significant_events.source_quote: The SPCA launched its Adoption Date App in February 2024. Likened by some as 'Tinder for pets,' it is a mobile app that highlights animals available for adoption to a much wider audience and was launched to a group of 200 people at a fun block party. The app now has well over 4,000 downloads. The Society hosted its first Adoption Date event in July 2023 at Cyberport, the largest offsite meet and greet.
significant_events.summary: Launched a mobile app in February 2024 to highlight animals available for adoption to a wider audience (over 4,000 downloads). Hosted first Adoption Date event in July 2023 at Cyberport.

significant_events.source_quote: The team also ran a workshop on the '3-3-3' rules of what to do after adopting a dog. Another major push has been the 'Bring Your Dog to Work' (BYDTW) programme. A website to accompany the BYDTW programme - which includes a fundraising component - was launched in October 2023.
significant_events.summary: Organized workshops on dog socialisation and adoption rules, and launched the 'Bring Your Dog to Work' (BYDTW) programme with a website in October 2023 to promote pet-friendly workplaces.

significant_events.source_quote: A key development for 2023-24 was the launch of Hong Kong's first-ever animal welfare immersive classroom hosted in the new Tsing Yi Centre. Introduced in March 2024, this interactive experience is a much more engaging way to teach children, through gamification and sensory stimulation, about respect for animals and the duty of care.
significant_events.summary: Launched Hong Kong's first-ever animal welfare immersive classroom at the Tsing Yi Centre in March 2024, using gamification and sensory stimulation to teach children about animal respect and care.

significant_events.source_quote: However, the team is making progress with the people and animals in Shenzhen where the SPCA has an advisory role to support an animal shelter that looks after hundreds of animals. It currently houses 100 cats and 40 dogs, with the Hong Kong team providing veterinary services to ensure every animal is in the best possible health.
significant_events.summary: Expanded work in Mainland China, providing expert knowledge to dog management departments, guidance on TNR programs, and advisory/veterinary support to a Shenzhen animal shelter housing 140 animals.

data.original_text: The PSPCA's extraordinary work benefits animals in need, pet parents, and residents throughout Pennsylvania, crossing all social and economic boundaries and age ranges.

In 2020, ASPCA launched a series of programs in response to the COVID-19 pandemic and its effect on pets, owners, and communities including free pet food for dogs, cats, and horses in New York City, Los Angeles, Miami, and Asheville, grants to animal welfare organizations, emergency pet boarding services, a New York City COVID-19 Pet Hotline, and expanded stationary and mobile veterinary care.
A Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (SPCA) is a common name for non-profit animal welfare organizations around the world.
The PSPCA rescues animals from cruelty and neglect, rehabilitates them medically and behaviorally, and places them with new, loving families.

We bring our mission to life through the lifesaving efforts of our Animal Law Enforcement division, in-house Shelter Hospital, low-cost veterinary services, behavior and enrichment program, humane education, and placement of abandoned animals in loving forever homes.
Among its many programs are a 24-hour animal poison control hotline, animal training and behavior modification, and mobile spay and neuter clinics.
Donations run programs and services including Cruelty Investigations, Disaster Animal Response Team, Violence Prevention Humane Education and a variety of shelter services.
SPCA International distributes critical veterinary medical supplies to animal welfare groups throughout the world.
We provide grants to small shelters, rescue organizations and advocacy groups to support their life-saving work.
SPCA International delivers important, actionable information to pet parents, animal activists and concerned citizens to help improve the lives of animals around the world.
The PSPCA has the distinction of being the state's oldest, largest and most comprehensive animal welfare organization, and the nation's second oldest.
We do not euthanize for time or space, and we are proud to have achieved a 97 percent live-release rate.
In its first two years, the partnership increased the number of animal cruelty arrests in the city by more than 200 percent.
In 1966, the ASPCA helped pass the Animal Welfare Act (AWA).

The ASPCA has also been a leader in animal welfare. It pioneered the idea of cage-free shelters such as Maddie's Pet Adoption in San Francisco, California, where the animals live in a home-like setting until they are adopted.
The ASPCA has rescued countless dogs and puppies from puppy mills and worked to place them in shelters and rescues where they can be fostered until they are ready to be adopted into homes.
In the rescue mission, ASPCA granted more than $13 million to shelters and organizations in the region to help with the resettlement.
SPCA International has distributed millions of dollars in veterinary supplies and medication to organizations on almost every continent.
In 2023, the ASPCA awarded nearly $7.5 million in grants to 393 organizations across 49 states and the District of Columbia.
In 2023, we had over 43,000 enrollments from more than 700 organizations worldwide, sharing much of this expertise on ASPCApro.org.
In 2023, more than 600 animals were helped at the center.

In 2023, the ASPCA Animal Hospital provided veterinary care and critical services, including over 1,700 surgeries, for more than 9,700 pets who were victims of cruelty and neglect or whose owners experienced financial challenges.

We partner with shelters and rescues across 40 states to move more than 20,000 animals each year from shelters in areas with high populations of homeless pets to destination shelters where there is a greater demand for adoptable animals.
Since 2014, the ASPCA Animal Relocation & Placement program has relocated more than 250,000 animals.
The ASPCA opens a dedicated Kitten Nursery in New York City, primarily for kittens relinquished to Animal Care Centers of NYC, saving 11,000 kittens over 10 years.
The ASPCA helps pass the Veterinary Medicine Mobility Act, which enables mobile and ambulatory veterinarians to offer lifesaving care to animals in crisis.
The ASPCA launches a Behavioral Rehabilitation Center pilot program for fearful dogs from puppy mills and hoarding situations.
successful adoption drives where hundreds of pets find forever homes within days!
Our puppy mill campaign has increased enforcement action and reduced the number of breeding dogs kept in U.S. Department of Agriculture-licensed mills by one-third. That's 32,000 dogs a year!
We've also driven the passage of seven state and nearly 500 local ordinances to stop the sale of puppies in stores.

Over the last half century, we've helped to substantially drive down the numbers of animals who have entered shelters and, in turn, those who are at risk of euthanasia, through education, sterilization and adoption initiatives.
But last year, South Korea passed a law to end the dog meat trade.
We have won more than 100 favorable rulings for animals and drafted and defended countless new state and local laws and regulations.
And through ballot measure campaigns in the U.S., we helped the pro-animal position prevail through direct votes in more than two-thirds of 61 initiatives.

name: Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals
